seoTitle: Garden Island vs Gawler Belt — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Garden Island and Gawler Belt. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Garden Island vs Gawler Belt: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Garden Island and Gawler Belt.
heading: Garden Island vs Gawler Belt — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Garden Island, South Australia and Gawler Belt, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Garden Island has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Gawler Belt (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Garden Island or Gawler Belt safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Garden Island has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Gawler Belt scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Garden Island scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Gawler Belt scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Garden Island or Gawler Belt?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Garden Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
